waves.theme: Blocking Runtime Defects — Process Control & Pipeline Condition Evaluation

items.checklist_item: 9.1.6 — Abort SIGTERM→SIGKILL sequence: TypeError on subprocess.Popen.wait()
items.description: In session.py _cleanup_on_abort() line 834, asyncio.create_task(proc.wait()) raises TypeError when proc is subprocess.Popen because synchronous .wait() returns int, not a coroutine. Fix: branch on isinstance(proc, asyncio.subprocess.Process); for Popen objects use asyncio.to_thread(proc.wait) instead. Remove stale TODO markers from the docstring at lines 790–796 to match the actual implementation state.

items.checklist_item: 9.11.5 — Graceful shutdown: kill running shell processes fails at same site as 9.1.6
items.description: Shares the identical _cleanup_on_abort() code path as 9.1.6. No separate code change is required beyond the Popen type-branch fix for 9.1.6. Verify with a test that abort during an active shell process reaches SIGKILL and does not raise TypeError.

items.checklist_item: 11.4.2 — goal_gate=true (boolean literal) never evaluates to satisfied
items.description: In conditions.py _evaluate_clause(), the bare-key path calls _resolve('true', vars) which returns '' (empty string) for any variable dict, so bool('') is always False. Fix: add a special-case guard before the _resolve() call — if key.lower() in ('true', '1', 'yes'): return True; if key.lower() in ('false', '0', 'no'): return False. Add unit tests for evaluate_condition('true', {}), evaluate_condition('false', {}), and evaluate_condition('1', {}).

items.checklist_item: 11.4.3 — goal_gate=true always redirects to retry_target causing infinite loop
items.description: Direct consequence of 11.4.2: evaluate_condition('true', ...) always returns False so the gate-failed branch in runner.py fires unconditionally, sending execution to retry_target on every attempt. Resolved entirely by the 11.4.2 fix in conditions.py. Verify with an integration test: a pipeline node with goal_gate=true and a retry_target must exit normally on the first attempt rather than looping.

items.checklist_item: 11.12.10 — Goal gate permanently blocks pipeline exit when using boolean literal form
items.description: Integration manifestation of 11.4.2: the blocking code path at runner.py lines 867–899 is always entered for goal_gate=true. Resolved by the 11.4.2 conditions.py fix. Add an integration test asserting that a pipeline node with goal_gate=true exits after a successful node outcome without hitting the retry redirect.

items.checklist_item: 11.12.11 — Goal gate never allows pipeline exit via boolean literal form
items.description: Integration manifestation of 11.4.2: the 'pass' exit path at runner.py lines 921–933 is unreachable when goal_gate uses the boolean literal 'true'. Resolved by the 11.4.2 fix. Covered by the same integration test added for 11.12.10.

items.checklist_item: 9.10.4 — SESSION_END not emitted on normal IDLE completion
items.description: session.py submit() finally block only emits SESSION_END when state is CLOSED (abort or auth-error paths). Normal turn completion transitions to IDLE and SESSION_END is never emitted. Fix: emit SESSION_END unconditionally at the end of each submit() finally block, removing the CLOSED-state guard at line 477. Update test_spec_compliance_final.py test_session_end_not_emitted_on_normal_turn which currently encodes and perpetuates the non-compliant behavior — replace it with a test asserting SESSION_END IS emitted after every turn.

items.checklist_item: 9.10.1 — ASSISTANT_TEXT_START/DELTA not emitted when model produces text alongside tool calls
items.description: In session.py _run_loop(), the ASSISTANT_TEXT_START / ASSISTANT_TEXT_DELTA / ASSISTANT_TEXT_END block is inside the 'not tool_calls' branch only. When the model returns both tool calls and interleaved text, the text events are silently dropped. Fix: check response.text before the tool_calls branch and emit text events unconditionally when text is present, regardless of whether tool_calls is populated. Ordering must be: TEXT_START → TEXT_DELTA → TEXT_END before tool call processing.

items.checklist_item: 8.1.6 — Middleware: deprecated constructor parameter never applied + stream() bypasses entire functional middleware chain
items.description: Two distinct gaps: (1) Client(middleware=...) at client.py lines 54–72 stores middleware in self._middleware but never consults it in complete() or stream() — fix by either removing the parameter entirely or routing it through apply_middleware() internally before the deprecation warning is retired; (2) _CallNextMiddlewareClient in middleware.py has no stream() method so stream() calls fall through __getattr__ to the underlying client, bypassing all registered middleware. Fix for gap 2: implement stream() in _CallNextMiddlewareClient using the same interceptor-chain construction as complete(), ensuring each middleware's intercept() can wrap both complete and stream paths.

waves.theme: API Naming Consistency, Edge Cases & Test Coverage Polish

items.checklist_item: 9.5.3 — Double truncation marker: redundant [output was truncated] appended after spec-compliant WARNING
items.description: registry.py lines 194–196 unconditionally appends '\n[output was truncated]' after truncate_output() already embeds the full spec-compliant WARNING message. The LLM receives two distinct truncation signals. Fix: remove the extra append in registry.py (lines 195–196). Add a regression test asserting the LLM-visible tool output contains exactly one truncation marker string after truncation fires.

items.checklist_item: 8.4.5 — All four adapters emit StreamEventKind.START instead of spec-required STREAM_START
items.description: All four adapters (openai.py line 589, anthropic.py line 612, gemini.py line 498, openai_compat.py line 129) emit StreamEventKind.START as the first streaming event. The spec requires STREAM_START. The accumulator in streaming.py line 69 already accepts both via pattern match as a backward-compat shim. Fix: update all four adapters to emit StreamEventKind.STREAM_START. After updating adapters, simplify the streaming.py case to accept only STREAM_START and remove the now-redundant START fallback. Also correct the factually inaccurate comment at streaming.py line 70 which falsely states adapters already emit STREAM_START.

items.checklist_item: 11.2.10 — Lint Diagnostic uses positional edge_index: int instead of a stable named string edge ID
items.description: validation.py Diagnostic dataclass has edge_index: int = -1 and all 15 lint rules populate it via enumerate loop position. Consumers cannot reference edge violations by stable name; positions shift if edges are reordered. Fix: add an edge_id: str = '' field to Diagnostic, ensure the Edge dataclass (or equivalent) carries a stable id or name attribute, and update all edge-diagnostic emit sites to populate edge_id. Retain edge_index as a deprecated field for backward compatibility during transition.

items.checklist_item: 9.1.4 — SessionConfig field named max_tool_rounds_per_turn instead of spec's max_tool_rounds_per_input
items.description: session.py line 83 declares max_tool_rounds_per_turn; spec §9 SessionConfig uses max_tool_rounds_per_input. No alias or compat shim exists for external callers. Fix: rename the field to max_tool_rounds_per_input and add a deprecated max_tool_rounds_per_turn property alias that emits DeprecationWarning and forwards to the new name. Update enforcement code at lines 523–536 to reference max_tool_rounds_per_input.

items.checklist_item: 8.9.40 — OpenAI cache_read_tokens > 0 on turn 2+: live test only asserts cumulative ratio, not per-turn turn-2 point assertion
items.description: TestOpenAICacheEfficiency in test_audit2_wave5_pipeline_hardening.py asserts cumulative cache_ratio > 0.50 across 5 turns but does not assert cache_read_tokens > 0 specifically on turn 2. The mock test in test_wave16a_mock_coverage.py covers this specific assertion. Fix: add a per-turn assertion after turn 2 completes: assert turn_usage.cache_read_tokens > 0. This closes the live coverage gap that the cumulative ratio assertion only indirectly implies. Mark xfail(strict=False) consistent with the existing test.

items.checklist_item: 8.9.42 — Gemini cache_read_tokens > 0 on turn 2+: live test only asserts cumulative ratio, not per-turn turn-2 point assertion
items.description: Identical gap to 8.9.40 but for the Gemini provider (TestGeminiCacheEfficiency). The cumulative 5-turn ratio assertion logically implies turn-2 caching but does not assert it directly. Fix: add a per-turn turn-2 assertion — assert turn_usage.cache_read_tokens > 0 — mirroring the mock coverage in test_wave16a_mock_coverage.py. Mark xfail(strict=False) since Gemini implicit caching is provider-side and may not fire for all accounts or regions.
